title: epipactis phyllanthes var. cambrensis
intro: Epipactis phyllanthes var. cambrensis, also known as the Welsh helleborine, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to the UK, particularly in Wales and the surrounding areas. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Welsh helleborine include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and soothe irritated airways, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
